108.status: Began a rehab assignment and played third base for Triple-A Salt Lake on March 31.
Knee started bothering him after March 15 outing. Angels will be cautious. Played catch March 26 and 27. Knee is improving. Throwing a bullpen is his next step.
Feeling good playing catch and doing strengthening exercises. Has yet to throw a bullpen since the injury but played catch in Houston on March 26 and 27.
Right middle finger nail fell off after March 17 start and hasn't improved much. Working through mechanical issues in Arizona and keeping his arm loose despite fingernail issue.
Progressing well in throwing program. Yet to throw bullpens at 100 percent intensity. Manager Kurt Suzuki said he looks good and wouldn't be surprised if he returns sooner than expected.
Was evaluated by Dr. Keith Meister in Texas and will have to determine his next step, general manager Perry Minasian said. There's at least a chance Stephenson could require another surgery.
Not going to play in 2026 and had the last year of his contract deferred over several years. Was placed on 60-day IL on March 24.

110.status: Threw multiple bullpen sessions late in Spring Training. Scheduled to begin rehab assignment at Triple-A Norfolk on April 1.
Underwent procedure to remove bone from right hand on Feb. 12. Began rehab assignment in Triple-A Norfolk's opener on March 27. Played again March 29.
Felt discomfort while warming up in bullpen on March 21. Underwent imaging in Baltimore on March 23. Playing catch and running as of March 29.
Sustained injury during March 19 Grapefruit League game. Underwent imaging in Baltimore on March 23. Placed on IL to begin season.
After testing, received platelet-rich plasma injection on Feb. 20. Has been running, fielding ground balls and swinging bat with left hand as of March 16.
Underwent surgery to repair torn rotator cuff and torn labrum last Aug. 19. Threw for first time March 3, tossing 25 pitches from 45 feet on flat ground.
Placed on the 60-day injured list on Feb. 14. No clear timeline for potential return yet.

111.status: Will throw four innings in extended spring training on March 30. Close to Minors rehab assignment.
Will throw four innings in extended spring training on March 30. Close to Minors rehab assignment.
Recently cleared a key hurdle when he was given clearance to run the bases and slide. Will stay at extended spring training until ready to go on Minors rehab assignment.

111.injury: Torn ulnar collateral ligament right elbow/right flexor pronator strain
111.status: Started gradual throwing program on flat ground in the second week of Spring Training.

112.status: Injured March 14 during Classic. Has advanced to on-field BP, plus outfield work and baserunning. Added sliding in workout on March 31. Scheduled for Minor League rehab (affiliate and duration TBD) during Cubs' upcoming road trip.
Resumed playing catch on March 3 after 10-day shutdown. Moved back to mound March 16. Could return to games by mid-April.
Shut down from throwing on March 8. Could be cleared to resume playing catch around April 1, per manager Craig Counsell.

Medically cleared Feb. 28 by Dr. Keith Meister, who performed Steele's elbow surgery (left ulnar collateral ligament revision repair) on April 18, 2025. Has advanced to facing hitters in live BP sessions.
Underwent a right patellar tendon debridement on Feb. 24. Cubs manager Craig Counsell said Austin could miss "months" while recovering.
Underwent surgery to repair a torn right UCL and flexor tendon last October. Signed a two-year deal with Chicago on Feb. 16 with an eye toward contributing in 2027.

113.status: Scheduled to throw in a second side session on March 31. If that goes well, Lodolo would make a rehab assignment start at Single-A Daytona on April 2. Then he could rejoin the club for a start during its April 6-9 series at Miami.
Threw on flat ground from 60 feet on March 29 and is scheduled to long toss at 75 feet on March 30. Ferguson will travel with the club on its first road trip.
Had arthroscopic surgery March 11 to remove bone chips and loose bodies from elbow. Shut down from throwing for six weeks; expected to miss 14-16 weeks.

114.status: Exited March 31 game in Los Angeles after fouling pitch off left foot in first inning. X-rays came back negative. Will take a planned off-day April 1 before team heads to Cleveland for home opener April 3.

Threw scoreless inning (13 pitches) in second rehab assignment with Triple-A Columbus on March 31. "We just need to make sure he’s built up and ready to go back-to-back [games], ready to go two out of three," manager Stephen Vogt said.
Began a rehab assignment with Triple-A Columbus on March 31.
Threw live batting practice on March 18. Has been throwing bullpen sessions and from 120-150 feet on flat ground.

115.status: Feltner was hit by a 106.3 mph comebacker off the bat of Andrés Giménez in the third inning of Tuesday’s game against the Blue Jays. He finished the frame, but was replaced by Juan Mejia in the fourth. This was Feltner’s first big league outing since April of last year, as his 2025 season was limited due to back and shoulder injuries.
Freeman was scheduled for live at-bats against pitching coach Alon Leichman ahead of the March 30 series opener in Toronto.
Moniak said he should be available for the April 3 home opener against the Phillies. He was set to start hitting during the series in Toronto.

115.expectedReturn: To be determined after he is cleared for game action
115.status: Faced hitters in a live batting practice session on March 28.

115.expectedReturn: TBD, owing to the trickiness of such injuries
115.status: Currently taking batting practice and engaging in baseball activities; scheduled for a Minor League rehab assignment beginning the week of March 30.

115.expectedReturn: Most likely headed to the Minors when cleared
115.status: Scheduled to begin an injury rehab assignment the week of March 30.
Dr. Keith Meister performed Tommy John surgery with an internal brace March 20 in Dallas. Petit, a Rule 5 Draft pick from the Tigers, will miss the season.
Underwent ligament transfer Tommy John surgery and internal brace procedure on March 4.
Targeting the middle of April to return to Minor League action.
There is no return in sight for Bryant, who has had a long and painful time since signing with the Rockies before the 2022 season.

116.status: Threw a live batting practice session on March 31.
Has thrown three batting practice sessions. Completing a bullpen session on March 31.
Started a return-to-play throwing program the week of March 30. Sustained a groin strain while doing a routine workout off the mound.
Playing catch out to 120 feet three times the week of March 30.
Completing a return-to-play throwing program that began on March 9. Expected to pitch again this season, according to team president of baseball operations Scott Harris.
Completing a return-to-play throwing program that began on March 12. Facing live pitching. Completing defensive drills.
Underwent surgery on Feb. 2, after offseason rehab program was unsuccessful.

117.injury: Torn ulnar collateral ligament in right elbow
117.status: Has progressed to throwing in the bullpen in West Palm Beach after undergoing Tommy John surgery to repair the ulnar collateral ligament with an internal brace as well as a repair of the flexor tendon in his forearm on June 6, 2025.

117.injury: Torn ulnar collateral ligament in right elbow
117.status: Has progressed to throwing in the bullpen in West Palm Beach after undergoing Tommy John surgery to repair the ulnar collateral ligament in his right elbow on May 23, 2025.
Has progressed to throwing in the bullpen while rehabbing in West Palm Beach.
Threw in the bullpen at Daikin Park prior to March 30 game vs. Red Sox.
Made his second appearance on Major League rehab assignment March 29 for Triple-A Sugar Land, throwing 1 1/3 scoreless innings. He threw 19 pitches (12 strikes).
Hader, who was shut down in early February, is throwing bullpens and should begin facing hitters in mid-April, according to GM Dana Brown.

117.injury: Torn ulnar collateral ligament in left elbow
117.status: Underwent Tommy John surgery on Sept. 23, 2025.

118.status: Hit by a comebacker in ninth inning March 28 and has been in a walking boot for two days. Played catch March 30 but isn't likely to be available out of the 'pen.
Suffered Grade 1+ oblique strain on Feb. 25 and began throwing bullpen sessions in mid-March. Threw two innings (34 pitches) March 28 in extended Spring Training.
Suffered calf strain on March 6 and left off Opening Day roster. Continuing to play extended spring camp games in Arizona and could go on rehab assignment early next week.
Missed all of 2025 after right elbow surgery, so took ramp-up this spring slow. Expected to continue it in Arizona before a rehab assignment.
After missing 2025 with shoulder issues, underwent right labral surgery in October. Rehabbing in Surprise, Ariz., during Spring Training but likely out for year.

119.status: Faced hitters for the first time on March 27 and will throw at least one more live session before beginning a rehab assignment.
Imaging revealed inflammation, but not structural damage, in Stone's shoulder. He has started a throwing program and essentially has an entire Spring Training rampup ahead.
Has not thrown since March 13, when he tweaked his side while throwing in the bullpen before a spring start. He will not begin a throwing program until he's pain-free.
Dealt with shoulder soreness during Spring Training. He progressed to throwing off the mound by the time the Dodgers broke camp, but the team is building him up slowly.
Slow-played his offseason throwing program due to lingering discomfort. He has resumed throwing bullpen sessions.
Velocity is down from where it is expected to be, so the Dodgers are slow-playing him. He is ramping up to greater distances in his catch play.
Underwent Tommy John surgery in June 2025 and could be a second-half option for the Dodgers.
Underwent Tommy John surgery in June 2025. He started throwing full bullpen sessions in mid-March.

120.status: Felt soreness pitching on March 20, has been shut down from throwing activity. Nationals are being cautious because Gray is returning from Tommy John surgery.
Schultz did not appear in a Spring Training game after Feb. 28.
Progressing through throwing program; threw a bullpen session on March 20.
Continuing throwing progression; throwing on flat ground at 125 feet.

120.playerName: Jarlin Susana (Nationals' No. 4 prospect, MLB No. 80)
120.status: Threw 15 pitches at a distance of 60 feet on Feb. 20; continuing throwing progressing, with flat ground throwing three times a week.

120.playerName: Travis Sykora (Nationals' No. 2 prospect, MLB No. 54)
120.status: Continuing throwing progression, including throwing on flat ground three times a week.

121.status: Is about a month behind other Mets pitchers. Will spend early April in Port St. Lucie rehabbing and pitching in camp games before advancing to an affiliate.
Exited the Mets' March 21 game after four innings due to knee discomfort and underwent an MRI, which revealed the tear. Will spend his recovery on the Minor League IL.
Fractured a rib during a March 5 game against the Nationals. Will need 4-8 weeks to recover, after which he can commence his buildup.
Will miss all of 2026 recovering from surgery but is under team control through 2029.
Will miss all of 2026 recovering from surgery but is under team control through 2027.
Will miss all of 2026 recovering from surgery but is under team control through 2029.

134.status: After "de-load" week, back to throwing sides and is expected to throw live batting practice early the week of March 23.

135.status: Scheduled to make a rehab start with Double-A San Antonio on April 4, his first game action since his 2025 season ended in June because of the injury. Could need a lengthy buildup progression.
Pitched 1 1/3 scoreless innings in a rehab outing with Triple-A El Paso on March 28. Eligible to return April 7; could return that day.
Hadn't built up enough to make the Opening Day roster. Returned toward the end of the Cactus League and pitched three scoreless innings in a Triple-A game on March 27.
Sustained the injury during the offseason, then aggravated it during a game on March 5. Began a rehab stint with Triple-A El Paso on March 27.
Missed the World Baseball Classic due to the injury, then began the season on the IL. Facing hitters at extended spring training in Arizona, could begin a rehab stint soon.
Missed the 2025 season but hoped to return early in '26. Made one spring start but experienced soreness afterward and was sidelined. Has resumed playing catch but has yet to throw off a mound.
Only appeared in five Cactus League games after sustaining the injury in early March. By the end of camp, had begun doing light drills.
Underwent surgery to repair the flexor tendon in his right elbow on March 17 and will miss the 2026 season.

Underwent an internal brace procedure on his right elbow on Oct. 29, which comes with a recovery timetable of 12-15 months. Will miss the 2026 season and was placed on the restricted list on March 25.
Underwent UCL internal brace surgery as well as a flexor tendon repair operation in April 2025. Has resumed throwing bullpens in Padres camp.

136.status: Rejoined team in Seattle on March 28 and is expected to begin Minor League rehab assignment as early as March 31. Could return during upcoming road trip through Anaheim and Arlington from April 3-8.More »
Suffered injury during catch play leading up to Opening Day. Team does not have timetable on return, though lat issues typically require rest, recovery and rehab.More »

Threw 30-pitch bullpen session with no issues on March 17. Manager Dan Wilson said Miller will throw “maybe another couple bullpen sessions where he’s not feeling it, and we’ll kind of go from there.”More »
Hurt his calf playing for Team Italy at World Baseball Classic. Wasn't ready to return to play by Opening Day
Underwent surgery to repair his right ulnar collateral ligament in January, and will be out all season.

137.status: Threw a 20-pitch live batting practice session in Arizona on March 30.
Threw a 21-pitch live batting practice session in Arizona on March 30.
Underwent season-ending Tommy John surgery with Dr. Keith Meister on March 25.
Suffered a high grade strain of his right hip flexor while pitching for Team Colombia on March 3 and is expected to miss up to three months.
Underwent surgery to repair the anterior capsule in his right shoulder in May 2025. Started a bullpen progression on Feb. 10 and is throwing twice per week.
Expected to miss 12-14 months after undergoing Tommy John surgery on Sept. 24, 2025. Was slated to begin a throwing progression in mid-March.
Expected to miss the entire 2026 season after undergoing Tommy John surgery in November 2025.

138.status: Pushard felt right knee soreness on March 30, per manager Oliver Marmol. After imaging, the team believed he would be unavailable for several days, triggering the move.
Will begin a 30-day rehab assignment with Triple-A Memphis on March 31. Expected to start against Norfolk.
Underwent surgery last October to address Haglund’s deformities in both heels. Started his running progression on March 20, but no date has been set for a return to the lineup.

139.status: Visited Dr. Koco Eaton on March 23 after feeling "OK" in last game. Traveled with team to St. Louis and Milwaukee, making about 15 throws in the infield per day in pregame work.

“Didn’t feel great” when playing catch Feb. 19. Received a cortisone injection on Feb. 20. Pitched in live BP on March 27, with another scheduled for March 31. Rehab assignment should begin soon after.

Threw a bullpen on March 23 and received an injection on March 25. Will be shut down for only two days before resuming throwing. "Expected to be a short absence," per baseball operations president Erik Neander.

139.injury: Rib tip syndrome, right oblique strain
139.status: Felt something in his ribs/ab/oblique after hitting on March 17. Underwent imaging on March 18. Was told he'll miss a minimum of 3-4 weeks.
Did not pitch all spring while dealing with a back issue that required an injection. Will resume throwing around April 6, but will need a while to build up after being shut down so long.
Underwent surgery to repair a right elbow flexor strain on Aug. 8. Hopes to return in June or July. Placed on 60-day IL on Feb. 10.

140.status: Threw 25 pitches during live batting practice at Globe Life Field on March 23. Manager Skip Schumaker called it an “exciting” development, noting Bradford looked in control of his pitches.
Took a comebacker off the bat of Kyle Higashioka in batting practice and will be out for about four weeks.
Signed to a Major League deal on Feb. 13, though is continuing to rehab from Tommy John surgery. Should be back on a big league mound around the Trade Deadline.

141.status: Doctors are still reviewing the MRI to reach a full diagnosis and determine if there is a tear, but Ponce will miss "significant time" according to John Schneider.
Will throw a sim game April 3 with roughly three innings and 45 pitches. Manager John Schneider has been "very encouraged" by his recent steps building up.
Scheduled to throw off a mound for the first time April 4, which will begin his ramp-up process.
Will throw another bullpen session March 31 at the club's complex in Florida.
Scheduled to throw a bullpen March 31, then face live hitters April 4.
Underwent surgery to repair the labral tear and is hoping to return to the Blue Jays in five to six months. This timeline should be clearer by June, but any return in 2026 would be a success.
Underwent surgery and will miss the entire 2026 season.

Was scheduled to begin throwing again March 10, which is a step in the right direction after being shut down the past couple of weeks. The Blue Jays were working to find some preventative measures to help Tiedemann avoid further elbow soreness in the future.

142.status: Threw bullpen session March 27, will throw live batting practice March 31.
Will throw a live batting practice this week and could progress to a rehabilitation assignment after that.
Is progressing in long toss program, expected to throw off a mound in mid-April.

142.injury: “Significant tearing” in UCL in right elbow
142.status: Underwent surgery on Feb. 25 to repair, rather than reconstruct, the UCL and will miss the season.More >

143.playerName: Aidan Miller (Phillies' No. 1 prospect, No. 21 overall)
143.status: Manager Rob Thomson said Miller’s “activities have increased,” but he still has not progressed enough from his lingering back injury to start swinging a bat. “We don't want to push him too fast and then all of a sudden, we go backwards,” Dave Dombrowski said on March 22.

Threw three innings in first rehab start with Triple-A Lehigh Valley on March 28. Expected to make next start on April 3 with Lehigh Valley, then make another with Double-A Reading the following week. On track for April return.
Threw a 1-2-3 inning in first rehab outing with Triple-A Lehigh Valley on March 28. Expected to progress to pitching in back-to-back games on March 30 and April 1.
Did not play this spring before being optioned to Minor League camp on March 7. Has been swinging at bat as of March 22.
Left Team Israel in the World Baseball Classic after feeling something in his abdomen/oblique. Phillies manager Rob Thomson said it’s “very mild.”

144.status: Strider will accompany the Braves on their road trip to throw bullpen sessions and possibly a live BP. The team hasn't decided whether he will need a rehab start before being activated.

Murphy will go to High-A Rome on April 1 to take live batting practice and possibly begin his rehab assignment. He will likely need a couple weeks to build his endurance. One of the last steps will be to catch nine innings on consecutive days.

144.injury: Torn tendon in right middle finger
144.status: Still hasn't faced live pitching, but the Braves are hoping he could begin a rehab assignment within the next couple weeks.
Could be available at some point after the All-Star break.
Underwent arthroscopic surgery on Feb. 23 to have loose bodies removed from his right elbow. Could return in June or July, but the team isn’t putting a timetable on it.
Underwent surgery on his right elbow on June 9.
Will miss the 2026 season after tearing his right ACL during a collision at first base during a Grapefruit League game on March 8.
Underwent arthroscopic surgery to remove bone spurs from his elbow on Feb. 18. There is a chance he could return in June or July, but the team isn’t putting a timetable on it.
Missed 2025 while recovering from left knee surgery and he had a cleanup procedure in November. There is no timetable for his return.

145.status: Exited March 14 start with right elbow soreness. Will undergo season-ending Tommy John surgery in the coming weeks; the procedure will be performed by Dr. Keith Meister.
Placed on 60-day IL as he continues to recover from Tommy John surgery he underwent last year. Has thrown numerous times off the mound.
Underwent successful surgery to repair the torn ACL on Oct. 28. Estimated recovery time is eight months, per the team.
Has been off the mound a few times and is feeling good in his recovery from Tommy John surgery he underwent March 7, 2025.
Continues with the bullpen portion of his recovery ramp-up, having thrown seven. Has felt great as the rehab process moves forward.
Hopes to return sometime after the April 2 home opener, but no official timetable. He’s with the team in Milwaukee
Hasn't played in a game since March 6 during a Cactus League contest. General manager Chris Getz said they will find out something soon in regard to Baldwin’s next move.

146.playerName: Thomas White (Marlins' No. 1 prospect, No. 15 overall)
146.status: Reassigned to Minor League camp on March 3. Felt discomfort during his Grapefruit League debut on Feb. 26.

146.playerName: Aiva Arquette (Marlins' No. 4 prospect, No. 45 overall)
146.status: Underwent core muscle surgery on Feb. 27. Sustained left groin injury during offseason workouts. Rested before arriving in Jupiter, where it flared up again.
Timeline of 4-6 weeks after being placed on IL on March 28 (retroactive to March 25). Scratched from March 27 lineup.

Performing baseball activities. Placed on IL on March 25 (retroactive to March 22). Will be reassessed in 3-4 weeks from March 22. Felt tightness on March 20 rounding third base. Missed 1-2 weeks with a strain of the same hamstring earlier this spring.
Placed on 10-day injured list on March 25 (retroactive to March 22). 6-8 week timeline from March 22. Replaced during third-inning at-bat after fouling off an 89.6 mph cutter.

Placed on 10-day injured list on March 25 (retroactive to March 22). Imaging revealed severity after being scratched from March 11 lineup. Stretched his side making a backhanded play during individual pregame work that day.

146.injury: UCL reconstruction with an internal brace (hybrid)
146.status: Placed on 60-day injured list on March 25. Underwent surgery, which has a recovery period of 13-14 months, on March 11. Experienced the discomfort on a pitch during his March 2 outing.

146.injury: Right elbow UCL reconstruction surgery with an associated internal brace
146.status: Placed on 60-day IL on Feb. 12. Underwent the hybrid procedure in December.

147.status: Threw approximately 50 pitches in live BP March 24, his fourth time facing live hitters since October surgery to remove loose bodies and shave a bone spur. Hamstring tightness March 31 could disrupt plans for a rehab assignment with Double-A Somerset.
Made two Grapefruit League starts. Will throw in game situations (and in the Minors) over the next several weeks. Will throw one 'up' of live BP April 1. Underwent Tommy John surgery in March 2025.

Hitting off a Trajekt machine and could advance to live ABs in Tampa, Fla., on or around April 1. Participating in all defensive and baserunning drills and began diving on his left shoulder in early March.
Spent most of Spring Training throwing on flat ground. Tentatively scheduled to begin throwing bullpen sessions in late March. Could face hitters in April or May.

158.status: Underwent surgery on March 30 and was already beginning physical therapy by March 31. The Brewers hope to have Vaughn back in the lineup by mid-May.
Sent to Triple-A Nashville on March 31 to begin rehab assignment.
Sent to Triple-A Nashville on March 31 to begin rehab assignment.

158.expectedReturn: No sooner than late April/early May
158.status: Threw an encouraging bullpen session on March 28 and will repeat once more before traveling to Arizona to face hitters
Aggravated a previous injury on a check swing during exhibition games against the Reds, and an MRI scan revealed a small fracture. (last updated: March 26)
Suffered right soleus injury on March 12. Aiming for April 15 return to games.
Injured running to first base on March 9, and an MRI revealed a more serious injury than initially believed.
A non-roster invitee to big league camp, Garabito had a bone fragment removed and a tendon repaired in his foot. He's expected to miss about four months.
A former top prospect who was in camp as a non-roster invitee, Garcia was injured playing winter ball.
